summary: No-ship: exact resume-by-id is dropped at the endpoint, and one hook/open race can still duplicate UUID-keyed sessions.

findings.title: POST /sessions/:id/ptys never passes the stored Claude session id to resume
findings.recommendation: Use found.session?.claudeSessionId, or add claudeSessionId to findSessionById's returned locator, before calling ensurePty. Add an endpoint-level regression where a UUID-keyed opened record resumes with --resume <that id>, not --continue.

findings.title: Concurrent hook and /sessions/open can still create a duplicate branch-keyed record
findings.recommendation: Move the match/re-read and update/create decision into one withStateWrite critical section, or make the locked writer re-check opts.claudeSessionId against latest state before falling back to cwd/branch auto-create. Add a race regression for openSessionByClaudeId and applyHookEvent for the same sid/cwd.
